tasks.title: Next.js project setup and dependencies
tasks.description: Initialise the Next.js 14 (App Router) project if it does not exist. Install all required packages: `poker-ts pokersolver @types/pokersolver drizzle-orm @neondatabase/serverless drizzle-kit next-auth@beta bcryptjs @types/bcryptjs framer-motion tailwindcss`. Configure Tailwind CSS. Create `.env.local` with placeholder keys: `DATABASE_URL`, `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`, `NEXTAUTH_URL`. Create `drizzle.config.ts` pointing to `lib/db/schema.ts` with the Neon driver. Ensure `tsconfig.json` has path alias `@/*` for the project root.

tasks.title: Database schema, Drizzle ORM, and NeonDB client
tasks.description:
Create `lib/db/schema.ts` defining two tables and one view with Drizzle ORM.

(1) `users` table: `id` (UUID PK, gen_random_uuid()), `username` (text, unique, not null), `password_hash` (text, not null), `balance` (integer, not null, default 400), `date_last_accessed` (date, not null, default CURRENT_DATE), `created_at` (timestamptz, not null, default now()). **No email column.**

(2) `game_sessions` table: `session_id` (UUID PK), `user_id` (UUID, not null, FK → users.id), `config` (JSONB, not null — stores `{ smallBlind, bigBlind, buyIn, numPlayers }`), `session_stack` (integer, not null — player's current chip value for this session), `created_at` (timestamptz, not null, default now()).

(3) `leaderboard` view: SELECT u.username, u.balance + COALESCE(gs.session_stack, 0) AS total_balance FROM users u LEFT JOIN game_sessions gs ON gs.user_id = u.id ORDER BY total_balance DESC LIMIT 10. This reflects each user's true total wealth including any active session stack.

Create `lib/db/index.ts` that exports a Drizzle client using `@neondatabase/serverless` connected via `process.env.DATABASE_URL`. Run `drizzle-kit generate` to create the initial migration, and `drizzle-kit migrate` or push to apply it.

tasks.title: NextAuth v5 credentials provider and auth route
tasks.description: Create `lib/auth.ts` configuring NextAuth v5 with a Credentials provider. The `authorize` function receives `{ username, password }` (no email — registration and login are username+password only), queries the DB for a user by username, and compares the password with `bcryptjs.compare`. On success return `{ id, username }`. Use JWT sessions. Export `{ handlers, auth, signIn, signOut }`. Create `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` that re-exports `handlers.GET` and `handlers.POST`. Configure `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` from env. The JWT should include the user's `id` and `username` so downstream server actions can verify the session.

tasks.title: Middleware for authenticated route protection
tasks.description: Create `middleware.ts` at the project root. Protect all routes except `/login` and `/register` (and the NextAuth API route `/api/auth`). Use the `auth` export from `lib/auth.ts`. If no session exists, redirect to `/login`. If a logged-in user visits `/login` or `/register`, redirect to `/`.

tasks.title: Balance and session-stack server actions
tasks.description:
Create `lib/actions/balance.ts` with `'use server'` directive. All functions must verify that `userId` matches the authenticated session user (call `auth()` from NextAuth) to prevent cross-user manipulation. Use Drizzle ORM for all DB operations.

Implement the following exported async functions:

(1) `recoverPriorSession(userId: string): Promise<{ recovered: boolean; amount: number }>` — checks if a `game_sessions` row exists for this user; if so, transfers its `session_stack` back to `users.balance` (`balance += session_stack`, delete the session row), returns `{ recovered: true, amount: session_stack }`; if no prior session, returns `{ recovered: false, amount: 0 }`. This is the primary recovery mechanism called on every lobby page load.

(2) `startGameSession(sessionId: string, userId: string, buyIn: number, config: { smallBlind: number; bigBlind: number; buyIn: number; numPlayers: number }): Promise<void>` — as a safety net, checks if a prior `game_sessions` row exists for this user; if so, transfers its `session_stack` back to `users.balance` first. Then deducts buy-in from `users.balance` (`balance -= buyIn`, reject if new balance would go negative). Inserts a new `game_sessions` row with `session_id = sessionId`, `user_id`, `config` (JSONB), `session_stack = buyIn`.

(3) `deductFromSession(sessionId: string, userId: string, amount: number): Promise<void>` — verifies `amount > 0`, verifies `session_stack - amount >= 0`, then decrements `game_sessions.session_stack` by `amount` WHERE `session_id = sessionId AND user_id = userId`.

(4) `creditToSession(sessionId: string, userId: string, amount: number): Promise<void>` — verifies `amount > 0`, increments `game_sessions.session_stack` by `amount` WHERE `session_id = sessionId AND user_id = userId`.

(5) `cashOut(sessionId: string, userId: string): Promise<{ finalBalance: number }>` — fetches `session_stack` from the session row, adds it to `users.balance`, deletes the session row, returns the new balance.

tasks.title: User server actions (register and daily reward)
tasks.description: Create `lib/actions/user.ts` with `'use server'` directive. Implement: (1) `createUser(data: { username: string; password: string }): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>` — no email field; validates that `username` and `password` are non-empty, hashes password with `bcryptjs.hash(password, 10)`, inserts into `users` table with `balance = 400` and `date_last_accessed = CURRENT_DATE`, returns `{ success: true }` or `{ success: false, error: 'Username already in use' }` on unique constraint violation. (2) `applyDailyReward(userId: string): Promise<{ rewarded: boolean }>` — compares `CURRENT_DATE > user.date_last_accessed`; if true, runs UPDATE to set `date_last_accessed = CURRENT_DATE` and, if `balance < 200`, also increments `balance += 200`; returns `{ rewarded: true }` if the date check passed (regardless of whether the balance was below 200), else `{ rewarded: false }`.

tasks.title: Game constants — tier configuration
tasks.description: Create `lib/game/constants.ts` exporting a `TIERS` array with three entries: Beginner (smallBlind: 1, bigBlind: 2, buyIn: 200, minBalance: 200, label: 'Beginner'), Specialist (smallBlind: 5, bigBlind: 10, buyIn: 1000, minBalance: 1000, label: 'Specialist'), Master (smallBlind: 25, bigBlind: 50, buyIn: 5000, minBalance: 5000, label: 'Master'). Also export a `TierConfig` TypeScript interface: `{ label: string; smallBlind: number; bigBlind: number; buyIn: number; minBalance: number }`. Buy-in is always 100× the big blind.

tasks.title: Deck logic — build and shuffle
tasks.description: Create `lib/game/deck.ts`. Export a `Card` interface: `{ rank: string; suit: string }` where rank is one of `['2','3','4','5','6','7','8','9','T','J','Q','K','A']` and suit is one of `['clubs','diamonds','hearts','spades']`. Export `buildAndShuffleDeck(): Card[]` — builds a 52-card deck by crossing all ranks with all suits, then applies the Fisher-Yates shuffle in-place, and returns the shuffled deck. The deck is built fresh each time (called once per hand). Export a helper `cardToSolverString(card: Card): string` that converts a card to pokersolver notation (e.g. `{ rank: 'A', suit: 'hearts' }` → `'Ah'`).

tasks.title: Bot logic — simple check/call strategy
tasks.description: Create `lib/game/bots.ts`. Export `takeBotAction(table: PokerTable, seat: number): Promise<void>` where `PokerTable` is the poker-ts Table type. The function waits 2000 ms (simulated thinking), reads `table.legalActions()`, and calls `table.actionTaken('check')` if `'check'` is in the actions list, otherwise calls `table.actionTaken('call')`. Bots never fold and never raise. Export `BOT_THINK_DELAY_MS = 2000` as a named constant.

tasks.title: pokersolver wrapper for hand evaluation
tasks.description: Create `lib/game/solver.ts`. Import `Hand` from `pokersolver`. Export `evaluateHands(hands: { seat: number; cards: string[] }[]): { winners: number[]; handNames: Record<number, string> }` — calls `Hand.solve(cards)` for each seat's card strings (pokersolver format: `'Ah'`, `'Kd'`, etc.), then `Hand.winners(solvedHands)` to determine the winner(s). Returns an array of winning seat indices and a map of seat → hand name (e.g. `'Full House'`). Export `evaluatePot(eligibleSeats: number[], holeCardsBySeat: Record<number, string[]>, communityCards: string[]): { winners: number[]; handNames: Record<number, string> }` that combines each seat's 2 hole cards + 5 community cards into 7 card strings before evaluating.

tasks.title: Server-side game session init action
tasks.description:
Create `lib/actions/session.ts` with `'use server'` directive. Implement `initGameSession(tierId: number, numPlayers: number): Promise<{ sessionId: string }>`. Steps: (1) Call `auth()` to get the current user's id and verify they are authenticated. (2) Look up the tier config from `TIERS[tierId]` (from `lib/game/constants.ts`). (3) Verify the user's `users.balance` meets `tier.minBalance`; throw an error if not. (4) Generate a UUID `sessionId`. (5) Call `startGameSession(sessionId, userId, tier.buyIn, { smallBlind, bigBlind, buyIn, numPlayers })` from `lib/actions/balance.ts` — this handles recovering any residual prior session as a safety net, deducting the buy-in from `users.balance`, and creating the `game_sessions` row. (6) Return `{ sessionId }`. The lobby page redirects to `/game/[sessionId]` after receiving the sessionId.

Also implement `getGameSessionConfig(sessionId: string): Promise<{ smallBlind: number; bigBlind: number; buyIn: number; numPlayers: number; sessionStack: number } | null>` — fetches the `game_sessions` row by `session_id`, verifies `user_id` matches the authenticated session, returns the config fields plus `session_stack`, or null if not found or if the session belongs to a different user.

tasks.title: Base UI components — Button and Toast
tasks.description: Create `components/ui/Button.tsx`: a reusable button with variants `primary` (gold/amber background, dark text), `secondary` (outlined), and `danger` (red). Accepts `disabled`, `onClick`, `children`, and `className` props. Disabled state uses reduced opacity and `cursor-not-allowed`. Create `components/ui/Toast.tsx`: a toast notification component that accepts `message: string` and `type: 'info' | 'success' | 'error'`. Renders as a floating overlay in the top-right corner with an auto-dismiss timer of 4 seconds. Use Framer Motion for slide-in/fade-out animation.

tasks.title: Navbar component with session and balance display
tasks.description: Create `components/ui/Navbar.tsx`: a top navigation bar shown on all authenticated pages. Displays the site name/logo on the left. On the right: user's username, current balance (integer coins), a link to `/leaderboard`, and a logout button that calls `signOut()` from NextAuth. Balance is passed as a prop (fetched server-side by the parent layout/page). Dark background with gold accents consistent with poker theme.

tasks.title: Login page
tasks.description: Create `app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`: a client component with a username + password form (no email field). On submit, calls NextAuth `signIn('credentials', { username, password, redirectTo: '/' })`. Display inline errors for wrong credentials. Include a link to `/register`. Style with dark background and gold accents matching the poker theme.

tasks.title: Registration page
tasks.description: Create `app/(auth)/register/page.tsx`: a client component with username and password fields only (no email field). On submit, calls the `createUser` server action. On `{ success: false, error }`, display the error inline. On `{ success: true }`, call `signIn('credentials', { username, password, redirectTo: '/' })` to auto-login. Validate client-side that both fields are non-empty before submitting. Include a link to `/login`.

tasks.title: Card component (face-up and face-down)
tasks.description: Create `components/game/Card.tsx`: a React component representing a single playing card. Props: `rank: string`, `suit: string`, `faceDown?: boolean`, `faded?: boolean` (for folded user cards), `className?: string`. Face-up: displays rank and suit symbol. Suits ♥ ♦ are red, ♠ ♣ are black. Face-down: shows a decorative back pattern (diamond cross-hatch in dark red). Card flip animation: CSS 3D `rotateY` transform over 300 ms using Framer Motion. Size: roughly 60×84px. The `faded` prop reduces opacity to ~50% for folded cards.

tasks.title: PlayerSeat component
tasks.description: Create `components/game/PlayerSeat.tsx`. Props: `seat: number`, `username: string`, `chips: number`, `currentBet: number`, `previousBet: number` (for delta display), `isActive: boolean` (current player to act), `isDealer: boolean`, `isSmallBlind: boolean`, `isBigBlind: boolean`, `isFolded: boolean`, `isBot: boolean`, `cards: Card[] | null` (null = face-down), `isEmpty: boolean`. Renders: avatar circle (first letter of username or bot icon), username label, chip stack count, bet badge (shows total bet this round and `+delta` from previous bet in smaller text beneath), dealer button chip (D) if `isDealer`, SB/BB label badges, pulsing ring animation when `isActive && isBot` (Framer Motion animate loop on the avatar border).

tasks.title: CommunityCards component
tasks.description: Create `components/game/CommunityCards.tsx`. Props: `cards: (Card | null)[]` — always length 5, nulls represent unrevealed slots. Renders 5 card slots in a row in the centre of the table. Unrevealed slots show a face-down placeholder card. When a card transitions from null to a Card value, trigger the flip animation (Framer Motion `rotateY` 300 ms). Cards are revealed progressively: 3 at once for the flop, then 1 each for turn and river.

tasks.title: PotDisplay component
tasks.description: Create `components/game/PotDisplay.tsx`. Props: `pots: { amount: number; label: string }[]` — main pot plus any side pots. Renders the total pot value centred above the community cards. When `pots` changes (a bet is committed), animate a chip token sliding from the betting seat toward the pot using Framer Motion. When the hand ends and a winner is determined, animate chips sliding from pot to winner seat. For now the inter-seat chip animations can be placeholder transitions (move from centre); the seat coordinate system will be wired up in the PokerTable component.

tasks.title: ActionPanel and BetControls components
tasks.description:
Create `components/game/ActionPanel.tsx` and `components/game/BetControls.tsx`. ActionPanel props: `legalActions: string[]`, `callAmount: number`, `chipRangeMin: number`, `chipRangeMax: number`, `pot: number`, `smallBlind: number`, `currentBet: number`, `isPending: boolean` (true while a server action is in-flight — disables all buttons to block further actions until the write completes), `actionError: string | null` (non-null shows an inline error message above the action buttons), `onFold: () => void`, `onCheck: () => void`, `onCall: () => void`, `onBet: (amount: number) => void`. Renders three primary buttons: Fold (always), Check (if 'check' in legalActions), Call [amount] (if 'call' in legalActions). All buttons are disabled when `isPending` is true. If `actionError` is non-null, display the error message inline until the user retries. Opens BetControls panel when 'bet' or 'raise' is in legalActions.

BetControls: shows six quick-bet buttons. Each button's value is computed as `Math.round((pot * fraction) / smallBlind) * smallBlind`, rounded to the nearest small blind increment. The six buttons and their fractions are: 1/4 Pot (×0.25), 1/3 Pot (×0.333), 1/2 Pot (×0.5), Full Pot (×1), 2× Pot (×2), All-In (user's stack = chipRangeMax). A quick-bet button is **blacked out** (fully visually disabled, not just greyed) if any of the following are true: (a) its computed value exceeds `chipRangeMax` (user cannot afford it), (b) its computed value < `chipRangeMin` (below legal minimum raise), or (c) its computed value ≤ `currentBet` (bet size already met or exceeded by current round bet). The All-In button is always enabled.

Slider: range `chipRangeMin` to `chipRangeMax`, step = `smallBlind` (minimum lot size). The slider must not allow values outside `[chipRangeMin, chipRangeMax]`; regions outside this range are inaccessible. Numeric input synced to slider. Confirm button labelled 'Bet [amount]' or 'Raise to [amount]' depending on whether 'bet' or 'raise' is the action. Panel slides up from the bottom using Framer Motion when it is the user's turn.

tasks.title: HandResult showdown overlay component
tasks.description: Create `components/game/HandResult.tsx`. Props: `winners: { seat: number; username: string; handName: string; amount: number }[]`, `allSeatsInHand: { seat: number; username: string; cards: Card[]; handName: string }[]`, `isFoldWin: boolean`, `onContinue: () => void`, `onLeave: () => void`. Behaviour: If `isFoldWin`: show a brief overlay 'Player X wins the pot' for 1 second, then call `onContinue` automatically (no user click needed). If showdown: flip all remaining players' cards face-up simultaneously (Framer Motion stagger), label each player's best hand beneath their cards, highlight winner(s) with a glow/pulse effect, animate pot chips sliding to winner. After 1.5 s, show a subtle 'Click anywhere to continue' prompt. Any click/tap calls `onContinue`. Also show a 'Leave Table' button that calls `onLeave`.

tasks.title: PokerTable layout component with polar seat positioning
tasks.description: Create `components/game/PokerTable.tsx`. Props: all PlayerSeat data as an array indexed by seat, plus communityCards, pots, and children for the action panel. Renders an elliptical table using a CSS-clipped div with felt-green radial gradient and a gold border ring. Seats are positioned absolutely around the ellipse using polar-coordinate math: for N total seats, seat i is at angle `(i / N) * 2π`, mapped to `(left, top)` percentages relative to the table container. Seat 0 (user) is always at the bottom centre. Renders PlayerSeat components at their computed positions. Community cards and pot display are centred. Passes seat pixel coordinates to PotDisplay for chip-slide animations.

tasks.title: Lobby components — TierCard and PlayerCountStepper
tasks.description: Create `components/lobby/TierCard.tsx`. Props: `tier: TierConfig`, `userBalance: number`, `numPlayers: number`, `onNumPlayersChange: (n: number) => void`, `onPlay: () => void`. Renders: tier name, blind structure (e.g. '1/2'), buy-in amount, a brief flavour description. If `userBalance < tier.minBalance`: grey out the card, disable the Play button, show a lock icon and tooltip 'Insufficient balance'. Otherwise enable the Play button. Includes the PlayerCountStepper inline. Create `components/lobby/PlayerCountStepper.tsx`: decrement/increment buttons, current value display, range 4–8 inclusive, default 6.

tasks.title: Core game engine — state, poker-ts loop, hand management
tasks.description:
Create the core game logic as a custom hook `lib/game/useGameEngine.ts`. Uses `poker-ts` for state machine, `buildAndShuffleDeck` for card ordering, and `takeBotAction` for bot turns. Hook receives `{ smallBlind, bigBlind, buyIn, numPlayers, sessionId, userId, initialSessionStack }` where `initialSessionStack` is the player's starting chip count loaded from `game_sessions.session_stack`.

GameState interface (from spec §7.2): table ref (useRef), phase ('waiting'|'betting'|'showdown'|'hand_over'|'session_over'), round ('preflop'|'flop'|'turn'|'river'), deck, communityCards (0–5), holeCards (indexed by seat), seats (chip count, current bet, status), pot, currentBet, lastRaiseDelta, winners, userSeat (always 0).

Game loop: initialise table with poker-ts (`new Table({ smallBlind, bigBlind }, numSeats)`), sitDown all players (user seat 0 with `initialSessionStack` chips, bots seats 1..N-1 all with `buyIn` chips), call `table.startHand()`. Loop: while `table.isBettingRoundInProgress()`: if `table.playerToAct() === 0` set phase to 'betting' (awaits user input); else schedule bot action via `takeBotAction` (2 s delay). After round: call `table.endBettingRound()`, reveal next community cards from deck array (flop=3, turn=1, river=1). On `table.areBettingRoundsCompleted()`: call showdown logic using `evaluatePot` from solver.ts, set winners, set phase to 'showdown'. Between hands: remove busted players via `table.standUp(seat)`. Determine session_over if user has 0 chips or is the last player. Hole cards are stored in React state from the moment they are dealt, so folded cards remain visible to the user (faded). Bot hole cards are kept in state but rendered face-down.

tasks.title: Per-action balance persistence — wire server actions into game engine
tasks.description:
Extend the game engine hook (from T24) to call balance server actions on every monetary action. The game engine operates against `game_sessions.session_stack` (not `users.balance` directly) for in-session writes.

Integration points:
(1) When the user takes a 'bet' or 'raise' action: compute the additional chips committed (totalBet − previousBet in this round) and call `deductFromSession(sessionId, userId, delta)`.
(2) When the user 'call's: call `deductFromSession(sessionId, userId, callAmount)`.
(3) When a hand ends and the user won a pot: call `creditToSession(sessionId, userId, winAmount)`.
(4) When the user cashes out (clicks 'Leave Table' or is eliminated with chips remaining): call `cashOut(sessionId, userId)` — this moves `session_stack` back to `users.balance`.

Error handling: Each server action call must be awaited before the game loop advances to the next state. If a server action throws (network error, DB timeout, validation failure), set an `actionError` string in the hook's state and do NOT advance the game state. Surface the error via the `actionError` field consumed by ActionPanel so the user sees an inline error message and all action buttons are disabled. The user must retry (re-submit the same action) to clear the error and continue. Do not auto-retry silently.

The player's chip stack displayed in the UI is sourced from the local game state (poker-ts Table), not re-fetched from the DB on every action.

tasks.title: Game page — assemble all components into /game/[sessionId]
tasks.description:
Create `app/game/[sessionId]/page.tsx` as a thin server wrapper that: (1) calls `getGameSessionConfig(sessionId)` from `lib/actions/session.ts`; if it returns null (session not found, already cashed out, or belongs to a different user), redirect to `/` with a URL search param `?error=invalid_session` so the lobby can display an error toast; (2) extracts `{ smallBlind, bigBlind, buyIn, numPlayers, sessionStack }` from the validated config; (3) renders a client component `GameClient` passing these values plus `sessionId` and `userId`.

The `GameClient` mounts the game engine hook (T24/T25) with these params, passing `initialSessionStack = sessionStack` so the user's chip count is initialised from the DB value.

Render: PokerTable (T22) receiving seat data from game state, community cards, and pots. HandResult overlay (T21) when phase is 'showdown' or 'hand_over'. ActionPanel (T20) slides up when phase is 'betting' and it is the user's turn. Session over screen ('You win!' or 'You're out.') when phase is 'session_over'. Between hands (phase 'hand_over'): show a 'Next Hand' button and a 'Leave Table' button. 'Leave Table' calls `cashOut` and redirects to `/`.

Note: a browser refresh on this page destroys all in-memory game state (the poker-ts table is not persisted). The page reload will call `getGameSessionConfig` again; since no active game was found mid-hand, it redirects to the lobby where the session stack is recovered.

tasks.title: Lobby page — tier selection and game start flow
tasks.description: Create `app/page.tsx` as a server component. On every load: (1) Call `recoverPriorSession(userId)` from `lib/actions/balance.ts` — this transfers any leftover `game_sessions.session_stack` back to `users.balance` and deletes the stale session row. This handles the case where the user force-quit a tab or refreshed the game page mid-hand. (2) Fetch the logged-in user's updated `{ balance, username }` from the DB (after recovery). (3) If the page URL contains `?error=invalid_session`, display an error toast ('Session not found or already expired'). (4) Call `applyDailyReward(userId)` and show a toast if a daily reward was applied. (5) Render the Navbar (T13) with the user's current balance. Below the navbar: three TierCard components (Beginner, Specialist, Master) arranged horizontally (or vertically on mobile). Each TierCard's `onPlay` handler calls the `initGameSession` server action (T11) with the tier index and selected player count; on success, redirect to `/game/[sessionId]`. Dark green felt background.

tasks.title: Root layout — daily reward check and navigation structure
tasks.description: Update `app/layout.tsx` to: (1) on every authenticated page load, call `applyDailyReward(userId)` and store the result in a cookie or pass it as a prop so the lobby can display the toast. (2) Include the global Tailwind stylesheet. (3) Wrap children in a shared layout div with the dark green background. The daily reward check should only run for authenticated users (check for session). The `+200` coin reward toast is displayed on the lobby page when the reward was applied.

tasks.title: Leaderboard page
tasks.description: Create `app/leaderboard/page.tsx` as a server component. Fetch the top 10 users by true total wealth using a query equivalent to: SELECT u.username, u.balance + COALESCE(gs.session_stack, 0) AS total_balance FROM users u LEFT JOIN game_sessions gs ON gs.user_id = u.id ORDER BY total_balance DESC LIMIT 10. This reflects each user's full wealth including any chips currently in an active game session. Pass `cache: 'no-store'` to disable caching. Render a table with columns: rank (1–10 with gold/silver/bronze medals for top 3), username, total balance (balance + session stack). Highlight the row matching the currently logged-in user's username with a distinct background colour if they appear in the top 10. If the logged-in user is not in the top 10, no additional row is shown for them. Render the Navbar (T13) at the top. Dark background, accent gold table styling consistent with lobby theme.
